evidence_slots.subject: software-visible register interface and test/control inputs
evidence_slots.operation: write, clear, reset, and use lock/access-control state
evidence_slots.object: reglk_ctrl, acc_ctrl, rst_1..rst_13, DMA core_lock_reg and DMA configuration registers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: lock/control registers should be authorized, sticky/non-clearable when intended, and enforced on downstream configuration writes; resets should not reopen unauthorized configuration windows
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level passes reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl to many wrappers. In dma_wrapper, acct_ctrl_i only gates en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. The observed write block uses if(en && we) with no reglk_ctrl_i check. core_lock_reg update allows a nonzero core_lock_reg to be cleared by a subsequent nonzero write, and reset condition including rst_8 clears all DMA registers including core_lock_reg.
evidence_slots.path: NoC DMA request -> dma_axilite_bridge -> dma_wrapper en/we/address/wdata -> core_lock_reg/start_reg/length_reg/source/dest registers; reglk_ctrl[8] is connected to dma_wrapper but not observed in write gating
evidence_slots.state_condition: After reset or rst_8 assertion, DMA configuration and core_lock_reg clear to zero. After core_lock_reg is set nonzero, a later nonzero write clears it. During rst_8, local lock state is erased.
evidence_slots.impact: Software-visible locks can be cleared or ignored for DMA configuration, enabling reconfiguration after a supposed lock and potential unauthorized DMA transfers.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI software requester configuring DMA
evidence_slots.operation: configure and trigger DMA read/write transfer
evidence_slots.object: memory regions addressed by DMA source_addr_* and dest_addr_*, DMA start/valid controls
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMA start/valid generation must be gated by PMP allow/range checks for source and destination and by access-control/lock authorization for configuration writes
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dma saves start/length/source/dest inputs in idle/done, then uses an FSM to check load and store ranges with pmp_addr_new and pmp_access_type_new. In the observed store check, pmp_allow_new is assigned pmp_data_allow instead of pmp_allow_reg && pmp_data_allow. The transition to CTRL_STORE uses (pmp_allow_reg && pmp_data_allow), but because pmp_allow_reg is overwritten during iterations, earlier denial can be lost if a later pmp_data_allow is true. Similar load path details are partially truncated, so complete source/dest coverage is uncertain. valid_new is asserted in CTRL_LOAD/CTRL_STORE/CTRL_DONE once the FSM reaches those states.
evidence_slots.path: AXI-lite interface -> dma_wrapper start/length/source/dest registers -> dma core start_i/source/dest/length -> pmp_addr_reg/pmp_access_type_reg -> pmp allow -> valid_o/valid_reg transfer indicators
evidence_slots.state_condition: Configuration can be sampled when dma_ctrl_reg is CTRL_IDLE or CTRL_DONE. Stale/overwritten pmp_allow_reg is a concern after address/length changes and during multi-address checks. The end condition uses pmp_check_ctr_reg == 32'hffffffff after decrementing, making zero/underflow length behavior relevant.
evidence_slots.impact: A DMA transfer may proceed after one denied address in a multi-beat range if later checked addresses are allowed, potentially permitting access outside PMP-authorized memory.

rtl_evidence.detail: DMA stores start/length/source/dest, pmp_addr_reg, pmp_access_type_reg, pmp_check_ctr_reg, pmp_allow_reg, valid_reg; declares pmp_data_allow and abort
input command registers are updated when dma_ctrl_reg is CTRL_IDLE or CTRL_DONE
CTRL_LOAD asserts VALID_LOAD, moves to CHECK_STORE, sets pmp_check_ctr_new=length_d, pmp_addr_new=0, and pmp_allow_new=1
CTRL_CHECK_STORE computes dest address + pmp_check_ctr_reg*4, sets ACCESS_WRITE, and assigns pmp_allow_new = pmp_data_allow rather than accumulating
on pmp_check_ctr_reg == 32'hffffffff, transition uses (pmp_allow_reg && pmp_data_allow) ? CTRL_STORE : CTRL_DONE
CTRL_STORE and CTRL_DONE set VALID_STORE/VALID_DONE in valid_reg

inspection_records.candidate_finding: DMA transfer progression uses PMP checks, but the observed pmp_allow_reg update overwrites rather than accumulates allow state, so only the most recent checked address may determine progression; start/config writes are separately only acct-gated at wrapper level.
inspection_records.notes: Load-side PMP logic was not fully visible in observations, but the provided store-side logic is sufficient for a candidate transfer-guard violation.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI software requester or external we_flag/test/control input
evidence_slots.object: DMA control/start, length, source_addr_lsb/msb, dest_addr_lsb/msb, core_lock_reg, done/end registers

evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dma_wrapper instantiates axi_lite_interface and gates en with acct_ctrl_i. The observed write side resets registers on reset/rst_8 and, otherwise, writes registers when en && we. No reglk_ctrl_i or core_lock_reg check is present in the observed write block. core_lock_reg is writable and clearable as described in the lock-sticky record. we_flag is a wrapper and dma core input, but available write-block evidence does not show it as a proper lock guard.
evidence_slots.path: dma_wrapper AXI register writes -> axi_lite_interface address/en_acct/we/wdata -> en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i -> write case for core_lock/start/length/source/dest/done/end -> dma core inputs

evidence_slots.impact: An authorized-at-accounting requester can modify DMA configuration after lock bits are asserted, and any external/test we_flag may represent an unresolved alternate control path.

evidence_slots.subject: DMA or core memory access checker using PMP
evidence_slots.object: PMP-protected memory regions checked by addr_i and allow_o
evidence_slots.expected_guard: PMP allow decision must correctly enforce configured permissions for matching address ranges, including no-match/default-deny where required and privilege/access-type distinctions
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: pmp instantiates entries, scans entries in increasing index, applies permissions if priv_lvl is not M or entry is locked, and defaults to allow for M-mode no-match and deny for non-M no-match. However pmp_entry NA4/NAPOT computation sets mask to a single shifted bit and compares (addr_i & mask) == base, rather than masking low address bits for a range. This likely causes false matches/non-matches and incorrect authorization.
evidence_slots.path: pmp.addr_i/conf_i/conf_addr_i -> pmp_entry.match_o vector -> pmp priority loop -> allow_o -> requester/DMA PMP use
evidence_slots.state_condition: No match defaults are explicit: M-mode allow, other modes deny. Overlapping entries use first match due to break. TOR boundary uses >= prev and < current. NA4/NAPOT boundary/range behavior is suspect. DMA supplies access_type read/write in its FSM; exact priv_lvl used by DMA PMP instance is not shown in observations.
evidence_slots.impact: PMP may authorize or deny addresses incorrectly for NA4/NAPOT regions, undermining DMA/memory permission enforcement over protected ranges.

rtl_evidence.detail: for each entry, pmp_entry receives addr_i, conf_addr_i[i], previous conf_addr, address mode, and outputs match[i]
allow_o initialized 0; first applicable matching entry controls allow based on access_type; no match allows M-mode and denies others
TOR match uses addr_i >= (prev << 2) && addr_i < (conf_addr_i << 2)
NA4/NAPOT sets size, mask = '1 << size, base = (conf_addr_i << 2) & mask, match_o = (addr_i & mask) == base
DMA store check sets pmp_addr_new to destination address and pmp_access_type_new to ACCESS_WRITE

inspection_records.candidate_finding: PMP pmp_entry NAPOT/NA4 matching appears incorrectly implemented with mask = '1 << size and base = (conf_addr_i << 2) & mask, which checks only one bit rather than a full range mask; this can produce incorrect allow_o decisions despite otherwise sensible priority/default behavior.
inspection_records.notes: Default/priority logic in pmp.sv appears deliberate; the candidate violation is specifically the range-mask calculation in pmp_entry.

evidence_slots.subject: debug module/JTAG DMI requester or external debug NoC requester
evidence_slots.operation: read/write memory or peripheral/debug address space via debug bus path
evidence_slots.object: debug module slave/register space and any system memory/peripheral path reachable through dm_master/dm_slave AXI signals
evidence_slots.expected_guard: any enabled debug bus-master path must be authorized and range-limited; if intentionally disabled, grants/valid/read-data tie-offs must make the path unreachable
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: AXI_BUS dm_master is connected through axi2mem to dm_slave_req/we/addr/data and through a debug NoC AXI-lite bridge to external buf_ariane_debug_noc2/noc3 ports. Response signals for a separate debug-module system-bus master path (dm_master_gnt/r_valid/r_rdata) are tied to zero, suggesting that path is disabled. But the NoC bridge still drives dm_master.aw/ar/w channels into axi2mem and dm_slave_* with no visible jtag_unlock, acc_ctrl, or range guard.
evidence_slots.path: buf_ariane_debug_noc2_* -> i_debug_axilite_bridge -> dm_master AXI aw/ar/w -> i_dm_axi2mem -> dm_slave_req/we/addr/wdata/rdata -> debug module slave/register path; debug module system-bus master response signals dm_master_gnt/r_valid/r_rdata are tied off
evidence_slots.state_condition: Tie-offs make debug-module initiated system-bus requests unlikely to complete, but reset/tie-off consistency for request generation is not fully visible. JTAG unlock state does not mediate the external debug NoC path in the shown connections.
evidence_slots.impact: External NoC debug requesters may access debug slave/register space without JTAG authorization or range limitation; debug bus-master disablement is incomplete as a security argument because another debug path remains active.

rtl_evidence.detail: declares dm_slave_req/we/addr/be/wdata/rdata and dm_master_req/add/we/wdata/be/gnt/r_valid/r_rdata
AXI_BUS dm_master is converted by i_dm_axi2mem into dm_slave_req/we/addr/be/wdata/rdata
i_debug_axilite_bridge connects external debug NoC ports to dm_master AXI aw/w/ar/r/b channels
dm_master_gnt, dm_master_r_valid, and dm_master_r_rdata are tied to zero for unsupported system bus accesses
dm_master AXI metadata is tied off but aw/ar/w valid/address/data remain connected via bridge
jtag_unlock is passed to reglk_wrapper; no evidence it gates debug NoC bridge

inspection_records.candidate_finding: The external debug NoC path reaches dm_master/dm_slave without visible jtag_unlock authorization, while the debug module's own system-bus master response path appears tied off; the NoC debug slave path remains reachable and unmediated by debug authorization.
inspection_records.notes: This distinguishes the tied-off debug-module master response path from the still-connected external debug NoC-to-dm_slave path.

11: // Author: Moritz Schneider, ETH Zurich
12: // Date: 2.10.2019
13: // Description: purely combinatorial PMP unit (with extraction for more complex configs such as NAPOT)
14: 
15: module pmp #(
16:     parameter int unsigned XLEN = 32,       // rv64: 64
17:     parameter int unsigned PMP_LEN = 32,    // rv64: 54
18:     parameter int unsigned NR_ENTRIES = 4
19: ) (
20:     // Input
21:     input logic [XLEN-1:0] addr_i,
22:     input riscv::pmp_access_t access_type_i,
23:     input riscv::priv_lvl_t priv_lvl_i,
24:     // Configuration
25:     input logic [NR_ENTRIES-1:0][PMP_LEN-1:0] conf_addr_i,
26:     input riscv::pmpcfg_t [NR_ENTRIES-1:0] conf_i,
27:     // Output
28:     output logic allow_o
29: );
30:     // if there are no PMPs we can always grant the access.
31:     if (NR_ENTRIES > 0) begin : gen_pmp
32:         logic [NR_ENTRIES-1:0] match;
33: 
34:         for (genvar i = 0; i < NR_ENTRIES; i++) begin
35:             pmp_entry #(
36:                 .XLEN    ( XLEN    ),
37:                 .PMP_LEN ( PMP_LEN )
38:             ) i_pmp_entry(
39:                 .addr_i           ( addr_i                         ),
40:                 .conf_addr_i      ( conf_addr_i[i]                 ),
41:                 .conf_addr_prev_i ( i == 0 ? '0 : conf_addr_i[i-1] ),
42:                 .conf_addr_mode_i ( conf_i[i].addr_mode            ),
43:                 .match_o          ( match[i]                       )
44:             );
45:         end
46: 
47:         always_comb begin
48:             int i;
49:             
50:             allow_o = 1'b0;
51:             for (i = 0; i < NR_ENTRIES; i++) begin
52:                 // either we are in S or U mode or the config is locked in which
53:                 // case it also applies in M mode
54:                 if (priv_lvl_i != riscv::PRIV_LVL_M || conf_i[i].locked) begin
55:                     if (match[i]) begin
56:                         if ((access_type_i & conf_i[i].access_type) != access_type_i) allow_o = 1'b0;
57:                         else allow_o = 1'b1;
58:                         break;
59:                     end
60:                 end
61:             end
62:             if (i == NR_ENTRIES) begin // no PMP entry matched the address
63:                 // allow all accesses from M-mode for no pmp match
64:                 if (priv_lvl_i == riscv::PRIV_LVL_M) allow_o = 1'b1;
65:                 // disallow accesses for all other modes
66:                 else allow_o = 1'b0;
67:             end
68:         end
69:     end else assign allow_o = 1'b1;
70: 
71:     `ifdef FORMAL
72:     always @(*) begin
73:         if(priv_lvl_i == riscv::PRIV_LVL_M) begin
74:             static logic no_locked = 1'b1;
75:             for (int i = 0; i < NR_ENTRIES; i++) begin
76:                 if (conf_i[i].locked && conf_i[i].addr_mode != riscv::OFF) begin
77:                     no_locked &= 1'b0;
78:                 end else no_locked &= 1'b1;
79:             end
80:             
81:             if (no_locked == 1'b1) assert(allow_o == 1'b1);
82:         end
83:     end
84:     `endif
85: endmodule

12: // Date: 2.10.2019
13: // Description: single PMP entry
14: 
15: module pmp_entry #(
16:     parameter int unsigned XLEN = 64,
17:     parameter int unsigned PMP_LEN = 54
18: ) (
19:     // Input
20:     input logic [XLEN-1:0] addr_i,
21: 
22:     // Configuration
23:     input logic [PMP_LEN-1:0] conf_addr_i,
24:     input logic [PMP_LEN-1:0] conf_addr_prev_i,
25:     input riscv::pmp_addr_mode_t conf_addr_mode_i,
26: 
27:     // Output
28:     output logic match_o
29: );
30:     logic [XLEN-1:0] conf_addr_n;
31:     logic [$clog2(XLEN)-1:0] trail_ones;
32:     assign conf_addr_n = ~conf_addr_i;
33:     lzc #(.WIDTH(XLEN), .MODE(1'b0)) i_lzc(
34:         .in_i    ( conf_addr_n ),
35:         .cnt_o   ( trail_ones  ),
36:         .empty_o (             )
37:     );
38: 
39:     always_comb begin
40:         case (conf_addr_mode_i)
41:             riscv::TOR:     begin
42:                 // check that the requested address is in between the two 
43:                 // configuration addresses
44:                 if (addr_i >= (conf_addr_prev_i << 2) && addr_i < (conf_addr_i << 2)) begin
45:                     match_o = 1'b1;
46:                 end else match_o = 1'b0;
47: 
48:                 `ifdef FORMAL
49:                 if (match_o == 0) begin
50:                     assert(addr_i >= (conf_addr_i << 2) || addr_i < (conf_addr_prev_i << 2));
51:                 end else begin
52:                     assert(addr_i < (conf_addr_i << 2) || addr_i >= (conf_addr_prev_i << 2));
53:                 end
54:                 `endif
55:             end
56:             riscv::NA4, riscv::NAPOT:   begin
57:                 logic [XLEN-1:0] base;
58:                 logic [XLEN-1:0] mask;
59:                 int unsigned size;
60: 
61:                 if (conf_addr_mode_i == riscv::NA4) size = 2;
62:                 else begin
63:                     // use the extracted trailing ones
64:                     size = trail_ones+3;
65:                 end
66: 
67:                 mask = '1 << size;
68:                 base = (conf_addr_i << 2) & mask;
69:                 match_o = (addr_i & mask) == base ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
70: 
71:                 `ifdef FORMAL
72:                 // size extract checks
73:                 assert(size >= 2);
74:                 if (conf_addr_mode_i == riscv::NAPOT) begin
75:                     assert(size > 2);
76:                     if (size < PMP_LEN) assert(conf_addr_i[size - 3] == 0);
77:                     for (int i = 0; i < PMP_LEN; i++) begin
78:                         if (size > 3 && i <= size - 4) begin
79:                             assert(conf_addr_i[i] == 1); // check that all the rest are ones
80:                         end
81:                     end
82:                 end
83: 
84:                 if (size < XLEN-1) begin
85:                     if (base + 2**size > base) begin // check for overflow
86:                         if (match_o == 0) begin
87:                             assert(addr_i >= base + 2**size || addr_i < base);
88:                         end else begin
89:                             assert(addr_i < base + 2**size && addr_i >= base);
90:                         end
91:                     end else begin
92:

12: // Date: 17/07/2017
13: // Description: AXI Lite compatible interface
14: //
15: 
16: module axi_lite_interface #(
17:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
18:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
19:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10
20: ) (
21:     input logic                       clk_i,    // Clock
22:     input logic                       rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
23: 
24:     input  ariane_axi::req_t          axi_req_i,
25:     output ariane_axi::resp_t         axi_resp_o,
26: 
27:     output logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_o,
28:     output logic                      en_o,        // transaction is valid
29:     output logic                      we_o,        // write
30:     input  logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_i,      // data
31:     output logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_o
32: );
33: 
34:     // The RLAST signal is not required, and is considered asserted for every transfer on the read data channel.
35:     enum logic [1:0] { IDLE, READ, WRITE, WRITE_B } state_q, state_d;
36:     // save the trans id, we will need it for reflection otherwise we are not plug compatible to the AXI standard
37:     logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0]   trans_id_n, trans_id_q;
38:     // address register
39:     logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_n,  address_q;
40: 
41:     // pass through read data on the read data channel
42:     assign axi_resp_o.r.data = data_i;
43:     // send back the transaction id we've latched
44:     assign axi_resp_o.r.id = trans_id_q;
45:     assign axi_resp_o.b.id = trans_id_q;
46:     // set r_last to one as defined by the AXI4 - Lite standard
47:     assign axi_resp_o.r.last = 1'b1;
48:     // we do not support any errors so set response flag to all zeros
49:     assign axi_resp_o.b.resp = 2'b0;
50:     assign axi_resp_o.r.resp = 2'b0;
51:     // output data which we want to write to the slave
52:     assign data_o = axi_req_i.w.data;
53:     // ------------------------
54:     // AXI4-Lite State Machine
55:     // ------------------------
56:     always_comb begin
57:         // default signal assignment
58:         state_d    = state_q;
59:         address_n  = address_q;
60:         trans_id_n = trans_id_q;
61: 
62:         // we'll answer a write request only if we got address and data
